techcrunch.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
techcrunch.summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

techcrunch.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
techcrunch.summary: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

techcrunch.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
techcrunch.summary: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

techcrunch.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
techcrunch.summary: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

techcrunch.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
techcrunch.summary: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

techcrunch.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
techcrunch.summary: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

techcrunch.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
techcrunch.summary: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

techcrunch.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
techcrunch.summary: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]

techcrunch.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
techcrunch.summary: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

techcrunch.title: Apple’s lawsuit couldn’t come at a worse time for OpenAI
techcrunch.summary: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]
